claim.statement: Confirmation and high-risk action copy must state the concrete consequence in plain language: what exactly will happen, to which object, whether it is reversible, and what is affected. Button labels should describe the action ('Delete 3 invoices') rather than generic 'OK/Yes'.

limitations: Effective copy is context-specific; the principle is general, the exact wording is not

do: Name the object and count, and state reversibility explicitly
Use action-specific button labels matching the verb in the prompt
Call out side effects (e.g. cascading deletes, shared resources)
avoid: Vague 'Are you sure?' with OK/Cancel only
Hiding the real consequence behind cheerful or ambiguous wording

strategies: rewrite dialog copy with object and consequence
